revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Princess Reishi]][[fa:شاهدخت ریشی]][[fr:Princesse Reishi]][[id:Putri Reishi]][[ja:令子内親王]][[sv:Prinsessan Reishi]][[th:เจ้าหญิงเรชิ]][[zh:令子內親王]] {{Infobox royalty |name = Princess Reishi |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] |reign = 1108–1134 |succession1 = [[:en:Grand empress dowager of Japan|Grand empress dowager of Japan]] |reign1 = 1134–1144 |consort = yes |spouse = |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] |mother = [[:en:Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)]] |birth_date = 1078 |birth_place = |death_date = {{death year and age|1144|1078}} |death_place = |burial_date = |burial_place = |}}
'''Princess Reishi''' (1078–1144) was a [[:en:Empress of Japan|Japanese Empress]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. She was appointed [[:en:Junbo-Ritsugō|honorary mother]] (''junbo'') of her nephew [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]] and served as empress during his reign. She held the title from 1108 through 1134.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |last=Venning |first=Timothy |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M2bFEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sukeko&pg=PT276 |title=A compendium of world sovereigns: Volume II Medieval |publisher=Routledge |year=2023 |isbn=978-1-032-36198-7}}</ref>
== Biography == She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)]]. Her father abdicated in favor of her brother in 1087. In 1107, her brother [[:en:Emperor Horikawa|Emperor Horikawa]] died and was succeeded by her four-year-old nephew, [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]]. She was appointed to serve ceremoniously as the empress of her nephew in 1108.
In 1130, she ordained as a Buddhist nun.<ref>{{cite web |title=令子内親王 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%A4%E5%AD%90%E5%86%85%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B-1121071 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |accessdate=2019-10-13 |language=Japanese}}</ref>
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Tokushi|Princess Tokushi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Tamako|Fujiwara no Tamako]] | years=1108–1134}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1027–1105)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Grand empress dowager of Japan|Grand empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Tashi]] | years=1134–1144}} {{s-end}}

[[en:Princess Reishi]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Princess Reishi]][[fa:شاهدخت ریشی]][[fr:Princesse Reishi]][[id:Putri Reishi]][[ja:令子内親王]][[sv:Prinsessan Reishi]][[th:เจ้าหญิงเรชิ]][[zh:令子內親王]]
{{Infobox royalty
|name            = Princess Reishi 
|succession      = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]]
|reign           = 1108–1134
|succession1     = [[:en:Grand empress dowager of Japan|Grand empress dowager of Japan]]
|reign1          = 1134–1144
|consort         = yes
|spouse          = 
|issue           = 
|royal house     = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]]
|father          = [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]]
|mother          = [[:en:Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)]]
|birth_date      = 1078
|birth_place     =
|death_date      = {{death year and age|1144|1078}}
|death_place     =
|burial_date     =
|burial_place    = 
|}}

'''Princess Reishi''' (1078–1144) was a [[:en:Empress of Japan|Japanese Empress]] during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].  She was appointed [[:en:Junbo-Ritsugō|honorary mother]] (''junbo'') of her nephew [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]] and served as empress during his reign. She held the title from 1108 through 1134.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |last=Venning |first=Timothy |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M2bFEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sukeko&pg=PT276 |title=A compendium of world sovereigns: Volume II Medieval |publisher=Routledge |year=2023 |isbn=978-1-032-36198-7}}</ref>

== Biography ==
She was the daughter of [[:en:Emperor Shirakawa|Emperor Shirakawa]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)]]. Her father abdicated in favor of her brother in 1087. In 1107, her brother [[:en:Emperor Horikawa|Emperor Horikawa]] died and was succeeded by her four-year-old nephew, [[:en:Emperor Toba|Emperor Toba]]. She was appointed to serve ceremoniously as the empress of her nephew in 1108.  

In 1130, she ordained as a Buddhist nun.<ref>{{cite web |title=令子内親王 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%A4%E5%AD%90%E5%86%85%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B-1121071 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |accessdate=2019-10-13 |language=Japanese}}</ref>

==Notes==
{{reflist}}

{{s-start}}
{{s-roy|jp}}
{{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Tokushi|Princess Tokushi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Fujiwara no Tamako|Fujiwara no Tamako]] | years=1108–1134}}
{{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1027–1105)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Grand empress dowager of Japan|Grand empress dowager of Japan]] | after=[[Fujiwara no Tashi]] | years=1134–1144}}
{{s-end}}

{{Grand empress dowagers of Japan}}

In 1130, she ordained as a Buddhist nun.<ref>{{cite web |title=令子内親王 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%A4%E5%AD%90%E5%86%85%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B-1121071 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |accessdate=2019-10-13 |language=Japanese}}</ref>

'''Princess Reishi''' (1078–1144) was a {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Japanese Empress}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.  She was appointed {{ill|Junbo-Ritsugō|en|Junbo-Ritsugō|WD=Q10895773|lt=honorary mother}} (''junbo'') of her nephew {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}} and served as empress during his reign. She held the title from 1108 through 1134.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |last=Venning |first=Timothy |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M2bFEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sukeko&pg=PT276 |title=A compendium of world sovereigns: Volume II Medieval |publisher=Routledge |year=2023 |isbn=978-1-032-36198-7}}</ref>

== Biography ==
She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|en|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|WD=Q5507520|lt=Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)}}. Her father abdicated in favor of her brother in 1087. In 1107, her brother {{ill|Emperor Horikawa|en|Emperor Horikawa|WD=Q464267|lt=Emperor Horikawa}} died and was succeeded by her four-year-old nephew, {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}}. She was appointed to serve ceremoniously as the empress of her nephew in 1108.  

In 1130, she ordained as a Buddhist nun.<ref>{{cite web |title=令子内親王 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%A4%E5%AD%90%E5%86%85%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B-1121071 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |accessdate=2019-10-13 |language=Japanese}}</ref>

'''Princess Reishi''' (1078–1144) was a {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Japanese Empress}} during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.  She was appointed {{ill|Junbo-Ritsugō|en|Junbo-Ritsugō|WD=Q10895773|lt=honorary mother}} (''junbo'') of her nephew {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}} and served as empress during his reign. She held the title from 1108 through 1134.<ref name=":3">{{Cite book |last=Venning |first=Timothy |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=M2bFEAAAQBAJ&dq=Sukeko&pg=PT276 |title=A compendium of world sovereigns: Volume II Medieval |publisher=Routledge |year=2023 |isbn=978-1-032-36198-7}}</ref>

== Biography ==
She was the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Shirakawa|en|Emperor Shirakawa|WD=Q60949|lt=Emperor Shirakawa}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|en|Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)|WD=Q5507520|lt=Fujiwara no Kenshi (1057–1084)}}. Her father abdicated in favor of her brother in 1087. In 1107, her brother {{ill|Emperor Horikawa|en|Emperor Horikawa|WD=Q464267|lt=Emperor Horikawa}} died and was succeeded by her four-year-old nephew, {{ill|Emperor Toba|en|Emperor Toba|WD=Q349284|lt=Emperor Toba}}. She was appointed to serve ceremoniously as the empress of her nephew in 1108.  

In 1130, she ordained as a Buddhist nun.<ref>{{cite web |title=令子内親王 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%A4%E5%AD%90%E5%86%85%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B-1121071 |website=コトバンク |publisher=The Asahi Shimbun Company |accessdate=2019-10-13 |language=Japanese}}</ref>
